body.processed:
This simple salad lets our stunning heirloom tomatoes shine. Use a variety of colors and shapes for a beautiful presentation. The key is using tomatoes at the peak of ripeness, the best olive oil you can find, and fresh basil picked moments before serving.

Instructions

  1. Slice tomatoes into 1/4-inch thick slices and arrange on a large platter
  2. Tear fresh mozzarella into pieces and tuck between tomato slices
  3. Scatter torn basil leaves over the top
  4. Drizzle generously with extra-virgin olive oil
  5. Sprinkle with flaky sea salt and freshly cracked black pepper
  6. Finish with a drizzle of aged balsamic vinegar
  7. Serve immediately at room temperature
body.summary: This simple salad lets our stunning heirloom tomatoes shine. Use a variety of colors and shapes for a beautiful presentation. The key is using tomatoes at the peak of ripeness, the best olive oil you

image.alt: Beautiful heirloom tomato caprese salad with fresh basil

nodes.title: Farm-Fresh Vegetable Frittata

body.processed:
This versatile frittata is the perfect way to showcase whatever is fresh from the farm stand. Use any combination of seasonal vegetables. This recipe is a favorite at our Farm Table Dinners and works beautifully for breakfast, brunch, or a light dinner.

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F
  2. Saute onion and garlic in olive oil in an oven-safe skillet until softened
  3. Add chopped seasonal vegetables and cook until just tender, about 5 minutes
  4. Whisk eggs with cream, salt, pepper, and herbs
  5. Pour egg mixture over vegetables in the skillet
  6. Sprinkle with grated cheese
  7. Cook on stovetop over medium heat for 3 minutes until edges begin to set
  8. Transfer to oven and bake for 15-18 minutes until golden and set in center
  9. Let cool 5 minutes before slicing into wedges
body.summary: This versatile frittata is the perfect way to showcase whatever is fresh from the farm stand. Use any combination of seasonal vegetables. This recipe is a favorite at our Farm Table Dinners and works

ingredients: 2 tbsp fresh herbs (chives, thyme, or parsley)

image.alt: Golden farm-fresh vegetable frittata in a cast iron skillet

body.processed:
This tender, fragrant cake showcases our raw wildflower honey paired with culinary lavender from the herb garden. It is a farm favorite that perfectly captures the flavors of a Willowbrook summer afternoon.

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F and butter a 9-inch round cake pan
  2. Steep dried lavender buds in warm milk for 10 minutes, then strain
  3. Cream butter and honey until light and fluffy
  4. Add eggs one at a time, then vanilla extract
  5. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt in a separate bowl
  6. Alternately add dry ingredients and lavender-infused milk to the batter
  7. Pour into prepared pan and bake for 30-35 minutes
  8. Cool in pan for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a wire rack
  9. Drizzle with a simple glaze of powdered sugar, honey, and lemon juice
body.summary: This tender, fragrant cake showcases our raw wildflower honey paired with culinary lavender from the herb garden. It is a farm favorite that perfectly captures the flavors of a Willowbrook summer afte

image.alt: Beautiful honey lavender cake with drizzled glaze
